news.headline: H.C. Wainwright reiterates Caribou Biosciences stock rating at Buy
news.summary: H.C. Wainwright analyst Robert Burns reiterated a Buy rating and $11.00 price target on Caribou Biosciences following the updated ANTLER/CaMMouflage data presented at EHA 2026. The reiteration reflects growing analyst confidence in the vispa-cel and CB-011 clinical pipeline.

news.headline: Caribou Biosciences Shareholders Decide Key Governance Matters
news.summary: At its June 17, 2026 virtual annual meeting, Caribou stockholders elected three Class II directors (Andrew Guggenhime, David Johnson, Nancy Whiting) and ratified Deloitte & Touche as auditor for fiscal 2026. Stockholders rejected a proposal to expand officer exculpation protections.

news.headline: Caribou Biosciences Reports Dose Escalation Durability Data for CB-011 at the 2026 European Hematology Association (EHA) Annual Meeting
news.summary: A single dose of CB-011, Caribou's off-the-shelf anti-BCMA CAR-T therapy, produced deep, durable responses in heavily pretreated, BCMA-naive multiple myeloma patients, with an 83% CR rate, 91% MRD negativity, and 50% of patients in complete response at 15 months. Initial dose-expansion data in BCMA-naive and BCMA-exposed patients is expected in H2 2026.

news.headline: Caribou Biosciences Reports Long-Term Vispa-cel Data in Second-line Large B Cell Lymphoma at EHA 2026
news.summary: A single dose of vispa-cel (formerly CB-010), Caribou's allogeneic anti-CD19 CAR-T therapy, produced durable responses in second-line large B-cell lymphoma patients: 82% ORR, 67% CR rate, and 17.1-month median progression-free survival, with a safety profile supporting outpatient and community-center administration. The data followed FDA alignment on the design of the pivotal ANTLER-3 phase 3 trial (~250 patients).

news.headline: Caribou Biosciences to Participate in the 2026 Jefferies Global Healthcare Conference
news.summary: Caribou announced that President and CEO Rachel Haurwitz would participate in a fireside chat at the 2026 Jefferies Global Healthcare Conference in New York on June 4, 2026, discussing the company's oncology CAR-T pipeline.

news.headline: Caribou Biosciences to Highlight Vispa-cel and CB-011 Programs During Oral Presentations at the 2026 European Hematology Association (EHA) Annual Meeting
news.summary: Caribou announced that two abstracts on vispa-cel and CB-011 were accepted for oral presentation at the EHA 2026 Annual Meeting (June 11-14, Stockholm), setting up the subsequent data readouts. This set the stage for the positive ANTLER and CaMMouflage updates released in June.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings and business update
catalysts.detail: Caribou is expected to report second-quarter 2026 financial results and provide a pipeline/cash-runway update; consensus estimates point to a report around August 11, 2026, though the company has not yet confirmed an exact date.

catalysts.label: CB-011 dose-expansion data (CaMMouflage trial)
catalysts.detail: Initial dose-expansion data for CB-011 in BCMA-naive and BCMA-exposed relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma patients is expected in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.label: ANTLER-3 pivotal trial funding decision / initiation
catalysts.detail: Following FDA alignment on the ANTLER-3 pivotal phase 3 trial design for vispa-cel (~250 CD19-naive second-line LBCL patients vs. standard of care), Caribou is exploring options (including potential partnering) to fully fund and initiate the trial; cash runway is guided into 2H 2027.

catalysts.label: Additional vispa-cel and CB-011 follow-up data at future medical conferences
catalysts.detail: Company has signaled further longer-follow-up data updates for both vispa-cel (ANTLER) and CB-011 (CaMMouflage) programs at upcoming hematology/oncology conferences later in 2026.

pulse: Caribou Biosciences is a clinical-stage CRISPR allogeneic cell-therapy company that has narrowed its pipeline to two oncology CAR-T programs, vispa-cel (formerly CB-010) for large B-cell lymphoma and CB-011 for multiple myeloma, after 2025 restructuring and layoffs. Momentum has been building through mid-2026: strong long-term ANTLER data for vispa-cel (82% ORR, 67% CR, 17.1-month mPFS) and durable CaMMouflage data for CB-011 (83% CR, 91% MRD-negativity) were presented at EHA in June 2026, alongside FDA alignment on the pivotal ANTLER-3 phase 3 trial design, driving reiterated Buy ratings from covering analysts. Near-term focus is on securing funding/partnering for the ANTLER-3 pivotal trial, further CB-011 dose-expansion data expected in H2 2026, and the next quarterly update (Q2 2026 results expected around early-to-mid August), with cash runway currently guided into 2H 2027.
